beats.activity: Wake up, coffee, check sourdough starter and feed it.
beats.reasoning: Maren starts her day at home in Redmond with her morning routine and sourdough maintenance—a grounding ritual that fits her personality and recent pattern.

beats.activity: Drive from Redmond to Ballard office via I-405.
beats.reasoning: Standard commute to Ballard; I-405 is reliable mid-morning, and Maren learned yesterday to avoid problematic routes. She leaves early enough to beat heat-of-day traffic.

beats.activity: Work on corridor planning documents and cost projections.
beats.reasoning: Deep focus work in the morning while it's cool; Maren tackles spreadsheets and planning before the peak heat arrives and air quality from wildfire smoke becomes more noticeable.

beats.activity: Lunch with a close colleague at a nearby spot; stay indoors to avoid midday heat and smoke.
beats.reasoning: Maren values time with close friends and prefers smaller, meaningful interactions; staying indoors during peak heat and air quality concerns aligns with her practical nature.

beats.activity: Afternoon work session: meetings, more corridor planning, and email catch-up.
beats.reasoning: Maren stays at the office through the hottest part of the day (91F) when air quality is worst; indoor, climate-controlled work is practical given the weather and wildfire smoke.

beats.activity: Drive from Ballard back toward Lake Union area; stop to pick up kayak from storage.
beats.reasoning: Maren has been dreaming of Lake Union kayaking; evening temps are cooling and she's future-oriented enough to grab her kayak before heading to the concert.

beats.activity: Paddle on Lake Union to cool down and enjoy the clear evening before sunset.
beats.reasoning: With heat breaking and air quality manageable at dusk, Maren kayaks—her favorite leisure activity—on the water she's been planning to revisit. This fits her mood and the clearing evening.

beats.activity: Return kayak to storage and walk to Gas Works Park for outdoor concert.
beats.reasoning: Maren avoids big crowds (per personality) but a free outdoor concert in perfect evening weather is hard to pass up; she times arrival as the crowd settles in around 6pm.

beats.activity: Enjoy live music and the view at Gas Works Park; socialize lightly with the crowd.
beats.reasoning: Clear skies, cool evening, and music on the park's iconic hill align perfectly with Maren's future-oriented, weather-aware planning; she soaks in the social energy without getting overwhelmed.

world_event_prompt: Hot, clear Wednesday hitting 91F by mid-afternoon. Free outdoor concert at Gas Works Park from 6pm draws big crowds. Moderate air quality from distant BC wildfire smoke.
